name: Avalara AvaTax API
what_it_does: Enterprise tax compliance platform providing real-time sales tax and VAT calculation, filing, and compliance across 12,000+ tax jurisdictions globally. Integrates with e-commerce and ERP systems.
use_cases: Real-time sales tax calculation at checkout in e-commerce agent workflows
Automated VAT compliance for international transactions
Tax document generation and nexus determination
Automated tax return filing across multiple jurisdictions
Exemption certificate management for B2B sales
not_for: Micro-businesses with minimal tax complexity (overkill and expensive)
Businesses with simple single-jurisdiction tax needs
Teams not ready for enterprise contract and pricing
best_when: An agent needs accurate, real-time tax calculation across multiple US states or international VAT jurisdictions, especially for e-commerce or SaaS with nexus in many states.
avoid_when: You're a small business in one state/country with simple tax needs — TaxJar or built-in Stripe Tax may suffice.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic authentication using AccountID as username and License Key as password, Base64 encoded. OAuth2 Bearer token also supported for certain endpoints. Simple key-based auth suitable for server-side agent use.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox environment free; 90-day trial for production
pricing.paid_tiers: Custom enterprise pricing; typically $200-$2000+/month depending on transaction volume
pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing — requires sales contact for production use. Not transparent pricing. Can be expensive for smaller businesses. Sandbox is fully free for development.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by contract; typically generous for enterprise accounts

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Avalara returns detailed error objects with severity (Success, Warning, Exception), code, type, message, and refersTo fields. Error documentation is comprehensive. Warnings (not just errors) are returned in transaction responses.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transactions can be created with a DocCode that must be unique. Attempting to create with duplicate DocCode returns the existing transaction (effectively idempotent with same code). Agents should use stable DocCodes.
known_agent_gotchas: Tax calculations are not free — each transaction call costs money in production
DocCode uniqueness required — agents must generate stable, unique transaction identifiers
Sandbox vs production environments are completely separate with different credentials
Address validation is a separate call from tax calculation — don't conflate them
Committed transactions cannot be voided easily — agents must carefully manage commit vs uncommit
Tax rules change frequently — cached calculations may become stale
Company code required in most requests — must match configured Avalara company
